speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been having some chest discomfort lately, especially when walking up stairs.

speaker: doctor
text: Thank you for coming in. Based on your symptoms, ECG, and stress test results from today, I’m diagnosing you with Coronary Artery Disease, effective as of April 5th.

speaker: patient
text: That’s concerning—I didn’t realize it had progressed this far. Any other signs I should watch for?

speaker: doctor
text: Your body temperature is normal at 36.5 °C—no sign of acute infection right now.

speaker: patient
text: Good to know. I did take my temperature at home this morning, and it was the same.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 73 mmHg—within acceptable range for today’s visit.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been monitoring my BP at home too. It’s usually around 118 over 72, so this seems consistent.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s check your systolic reading now… okay, it’s 102 mmHg—slightly lower than usual, but not alarming given you just sat down.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, I’ve had a dull headache since this morning—it’s persistent but not severe.

speaker: doctor
text: Noted. Your systolic blood pressure is 102 mmHg—let’s recheck in a few minutes after you’ve rested a bit more.

speaker: patient
text: Sure. I also felt a little warm earlier—like I might be coming down with something.

speaker: doctor
text: Interesting—your systolic pressure is now 126 mmHg, which is higher than the reading we got earlier. Let’s keep an eye on that fluctuation.

speaker: doctor
text: And your temperature has risen to 37.9 °C—mild low-grade fever. That may help explain the headache and fatigue.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve felt achy and a bit off all day. Could this be related to my heart condition?

speaker: doctor
text: Possibly—but the fever and headache suggest an intercurrent issue, like a viral illness. We’ll rule out infection first.

speaker: patient
text: Makes sense. I took my temperature again about 20 minutes ago, and it was back to 36.5 °C—the same as that reading from earlier.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s helpful—so the fever appears transient. Given the fluctuating BP and new symptoms, I’m going to hold off on intensifying your beta-blocker for now and add acetaminophen PRN for the headache and fever.

speaker: patient
text: Okay, I’ll use it as needed. Should I still take my usual metoprolol this evening?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, continue metoprolol as prescribed—but if your systolic stays above 130 or you develop dizziness or worsening chest pressure, call the office immediately.

speaker: patient
text: Will do. Thanks, Doctor—I’ll monitor everything closely and follow up next week.
